query_normalized: the impact of social comparison on self-perception in ai-generated image platforms psychology
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (b) and (e) by measuring the psychological impact of algorithmic curation and "curated online personas" (the mechanism of social comparison) on a specific empirical population (teenagers) within the domain of social media platforms, which serves as the foundational context for studying AI-generated image effects. Although it does not explicitly isolate "AI-generated images" as the sole variable, it establishes the canonical baseline for how algorithmic presentation
